title: Implement WebSocket Server for Real-Time Updates
description: Create a local WebSocket server in the Rust backend that broadcasts state changes to connected frontend clients. Events include: ticket_updated, agent_spawned, agent_completed, agent_output, session_changed. The frontend subscribes on mount and updates React state accordingly. This gives us that sweet real-time reactivity without polling like some peasant.
acceptance_criteria: WebSocket server starts on app launch at ws://localhost:PORT
Frontend establishes connection and receives heartbeat
Server broadcasts JSON events with type and payload fields
Frontend reconnects automatically if connection drops
Multiple frontend windows can connect simultaneously
Events are debounced to prevent flooding (max 60 events/sec)
agent_output: WebSocket server on ws://127.0.0.1:9137 with tokio-tungstenite. Supports session events (started/output/tool/completed/error), ticket events, and heartbeat. Broadcast channel for real-time updates.
